objective: Deepen MLX porting evidence beyond GitHub with subagent research, non-GitHub sampling, and auditable blogs

adopted.title: Custom Metal kernels need official API plus local fallback gates
adopted.summary: The official MLX docs expose custom Metal kernel surfaces, but the skill should keep treating every custom kernel as mode-scoped until a readable fallback, parity oracle, and end-to-end benchmark exist.
adopted.validation_gate: Pin MLX version, keep a pure-MLX or NumPy fallback, compare kernel outputs against an oracle, and benchmark end-to-end before claiming speedup.
caveats: Official API support does not prove a specific model port benefits.
adopted.required_next_validation: Keep each custom kernel finding tied to mode coverage, fallback, local parity tests, and model-level benchmark evidence.

adopted.title: MLX memory APIs should back fit claims
adopted.summary: MLX exposes peak-memory measurement and a graph-evaluation memory limit API, so model-fit claims should include peak memory and budget-pressure experiments instead of relying on a single successful run.
adopted.validation_gate: Record active/cache/peak memory and a memory-limit experiment when the port claims to fit a target Mac.
caveats: set_memory_limit is not a latency or quality proof.
adopted.required_next_validation: Add live benchmark examples only when a real port run provides raw memory counters.

adopted.title: .mlxfn export is an optional shape/dtype-specific smoke gate
adopted.summary: MLX function export can support a release smoke test, but exported behavior is tied to example shapes/dtypes unless shapeless export is used deliberately.
adopted.validation_gate: Record example input shapes, dtypes, enclosed arrays, and shapeless-export choice; load and run the export as a smoke test.
caveats: .mlxfn is not a replacement for normal Python/config/weight artifacts.
adopted.required_next_validation: Use only for ports with a stable callable inference surface.

adopted.title: Compiled decode state needs explicit stale-state checks
adopted.summary: The MLX compile documentation makes shape/type/input-count and captured-state behavior important enough to test concretely in compiled decode loops.
adopted.validation_gate: Run multiple lengths or chunk sizes, verify consecutive state/cache mutation, and test refreshed captured settings.
caveats: The skill already had general compile guidance; this finding only made it more testable.
adopted.required_next_validation: Add a concrete compiled decode fixture if the repo later ships runtime examples.

adopted.title: Hugging Face MLX publications need model-card linting
adopted.summary: MLX model cards expose mutable but important metadata such as library name, tags, base model, license, quantization, and custom-code/package requirements.
adopted.validation_gate: Check library tags, base model, license, quantization metadata, custom-code requirement, and minimum package versions before publication.
caveats: HF cards are mutable and community-maintained; they are metadata evidence, not benchmark proof.
adopted.required_next_validation: Add a model-card lint script only when publication automation is introduced.

adopted.title: Package registries should be sampled in research loops
adopted.summary: Current PyPI metadata exposes active releases for mlx, mlx-lm, mlx-vlm, and mlx-audio; the skill should capture package versions during research so guidance does not silently rely on stale CLI or API assumptions.
adopted.validation_gate: Record package name, version, release date when available, and affected CLI/API surface before changing commands or compatibility guidance.
caveats: Package metadata does not prove behavior or backward compatibility.
adopted.required_next_validation: Add package-version fields to future live collector records when network execution is implemented.

adopted.title: Batched cache isolation needs explicit contamination tests
adopted.summary: mlx-lm package metadata includes a yanked release note for batched KV-cache cross contamination, which is enough to require mixed-batch isolation tests in serving guidance.
adopted.validation_gate: Test mixed prompts, shared prefixes, divergent suffixes, cancellation, reset, and cache reuse in the same batch.
caveats: PyPI gives the failure label, not root-cause details.
adopted.required_next_validation: Add a concrete batch-cache regression when a serving harness exists.

adopted.title: Audio ports need optional dependency and frontend matrices
adopted.summary: mlx-audio package metadata names optional language/frontend and compression dependencies, so audio and ASR runbooks should capture dependency-driven frontend differences.
adopted.validation_gate: Record optional dependency paths and compare frontend/postprocessing output for every advertised install extra.
caveats: PyPI project descriptions are not independently verified by PyPI.
adopted.required_next_validation: Validate per model and language path.

adopted.title: VLM package metadata should tighten cache and speculative gates
adopted.summary: mlx-vlm package metadata describes path-keyed vision feature caching, speculative decoding pairings/windows, thinking budgets, and TurboQuant caveats; the skill should retain stronger cache keys and require acceptance/window/tokenizer gates.
adopted.validation_gate: Use content/processor/model-hash cache keys, test speculative acceptance and drafter windows, capture thinking delimiter IDs, and keep TurboQuant batch caveats attached.
caveats: Package feature descriptions are leads until checked against pinned implementation source and local workloads.
adopted.required_next_validation: Pin MLX-VLM source surfaces before upgrading any technique status.

adopted.title: Dynamic research harness should scaffold prompts and record execution state
adopted.summary: A separate review artifact lane fits the repo better than automatic asset mutation: default runs should scaffold prompts and mark agents not run, while explicit returned findings are ingested with blogs and synthesis.
adopted.validation_gate: Harness tests must prove scaffolded_not_run versus fixture_ingested states, review-only policy, blog generation, and malformed finding rejection.
caveats: The script does not spawn host subagents by itself; the host agent performs live delegation and records returned findings.
adopted.required_next_validation: Add executor subcommands only when the repo needs to run child processes directly.

held.title: Technical blog claims need benchmark protocol capture
held.summary: Technical posts can reveal MLX deployment or optimization tactics, but the skill should not ingest speed claims unless hardware, software versions, workload, baseline, and quality gates are recorded.
held.validation_gate: Identify specific posts, extract exact benchmark protocol, and reproduce or convert into a validation checklist before changing optimization guidance.
caveats: A broad blog index is not a concrete implementation source.
held.required_next_validation: Collect specific MLX-relevant blog posts and attach benchmark metadata.

needs_validation.title: Apple Silicon serving papers should feed validation-first serving research
needs_validation.summary: The vllm-mlx paper reports continuous batching and content-based vision prefix caching on Apple Silicon, which is relevant to MLX serving guidance but still needs implementation-level review and local reproduction before promotion.

sources.title: Native LLM and MLLM Inference at Scale on Apple Silicon

needs_validation.validation_gate: Pin source implementation, reproduce a text and multimodal serving benchmark, and check quality/cache correctness before promoting serving guidance.
caveats: Paper benchmarks are source-reported and workload-specific.
needs_validation.required_next_validation: Deep-review the implementation surfaces and add local serving/correctness gates before any supported technique upgrade.

needs_validation.title: Hugging Face MLX metadata should drive coverage priorities, not support claims
needs_validation.summary: Current Hugging Face metadata shows heavily downloaded MLX-tagged quantized multimodal models and MLX ASR models, so the skill should use HF as a demand and artifact-shape sampler while requiring separate implementation validation.
needs_validation.validation_gate: For each prioritized family, sample model card, license, tokenizer/processor files, config, source framework, and source oracle requirements.
caveats: Downloads and tags do not prove source-preserving port feasibility.
needs_validation.required_next_validation: Turn top HF demand clusters into representative golden scenarios or runbook gaps.

needs_validation.title: Golden scenarios cover only 3 of 14 declared architecture families
needs_validation.summary: The skill declares 14 architecture families, but the golden scenario harness covers dense decoder, MoE decoder, and neural audio codec only; comprehensive MLX porting needs scenario gates for every declared family.
needs_validation.validation_gate: Each declared family gets a fixture that routes correctly, proves source weight keys, catches a seeded parity bug, and includes or excludes a family-specific optimization.
caveats: This is validation debt, not a direct porting technique.
needs_validation.required_next_validation: Create a follow-up task graph for golden scenarios across the 11 uncovered families.

needs_validation.title: Source-format coverage should expand beyond PyTorch and Hugging Face
needs_validation.summary: The front-door skill description and intake flow emphasize PyTorch and Hugging Face; comprehensive MLX porting also needs static intake for ONNX, JAX/Flax, TensorFlow/Keras, GGUF, Core ML, and checkpoint-only repos.
needs_validation.validation_gate: Add static manifest extraction without import/execution, framework layout conventions, unsupported-op reporting, and deterministic weight-map coverage for each source format.
caveats: Adding formats changes intake scope and needs careful unsupported-path reporting.
needs_validation.required_next_validation: Shape a source-format intake workstream with fixtures for at least ONNX and JAX/Flax first.

needs_validation.title: Non-generative CV, structured/time-series/recsys, graph/scientific ML, and training need dedicated tracks
needs_validation.summary: Contributor mining did not cover several important MLX porting domains. The skill should add research tracks for non-generative computer vision, structured and time-series models, graph/scientific ML, and training/fine-tuning as port targets.

sources.title: Subagent coverage audit for MLX porting skill

needs_validation.validation_gate: Each new track needs an architecture/runbook entry, model-support row, quality metrics, static inspection fixtures, and task-specific parity gates.
caveats: This is too broad for one promotion; split into follow-up workstreams.
needs_validation.required_next_validation: Prioritize CV and source-format intake before adding lower-demand families.

limitations: This loop used live host subagents for repo-local audit and harness architecture, but source-collection automation is still scaffold/fixture-based.
The Hugging Face result was gathered through the installed Hugging Face connector; detailed model-card parsing remains a follow-up.
The arXiv source was sampled through the arXiv API and should be deep-reviewed before any serving technique promotion.
Some guessed MLX doc URLs returned 404 and were excluded from evidence.
instructions: This synthesis is review material, not an automatic recommendation merge.
Promote findings only through explicit source, validation, tests, and manifest updates.
Do not execute remote model code while investigating candidates.
